This is a generated GraphQL artifact for the `ctf-product-table` component, part of the marketing web app template. Rather than defining its own data shapes, it composes type information and query fragments from three related generated modules: `ctf-product`, `ctf-asset`, and `ctf-product-feature`. This pattern indicates that a product table rendered in the app pulls together product data, associated media assets, and individual product feature details, all defined and validated elsewhere in the codebase.

Specifically, the file imports both the fragment type (`*FieldsFragment`) and the corresponding GraphQL document (`*FieldsFragmentDoc`) for each of the three related concepts. The fragment types give the component strongly-typed access to the underlying data shape, while the fragment documents are used to compose these fields into the GraphQL queries or mutations that power the product table. This separation of type and document imports is typical of code generated by GraphQL codegen tooling, allowing the product table component to stay in sync with schema changes in the product, asset, and product feature fragments without manually duplicating field definitions.

# Relations

- [Ctf Product.Generated](ctf-product.generated.md) — Uses product data typing for table rows {kind: sync}
- [Ctf Asset.Generated](ctf-asset.generated.md) — Uses asset data typing for product images {kind: sync}
- [Ctf Product Feature.Generated](ctf-product-feature.generated.md) — Uses product feature data typing for feature listings {kind: sync}
- [Ctf Product.Generated](ctf-product.generated.md) — Composes product fields into the table's query {kind: sync}
- [Ctf Asset.Generated](ctf-asset.generated.md) — Composes asset fields into the table's query {kind: sync}
- [Ctf Product Feature.Generated](ctf-product-feature.generated.md) — Composes product feature fields into the table's query {kind: sync}
